evidence_quotes.quote: even pure single-player titles are equipped with online compulsion as a feature, so that the control remains available at all times to de facto steal the product back from the buyer without presenting reasons
a fully-fledged purchase had not been disguised as a license lease in opaque EULAs afterwards
the least result should be that publishers clearly communicate whether they are acquiring a product in the sense of a legally valid purchase contract or if it is only a leasing contract.

paragraphs.text: Like many others, video games are very close to my heart. For this reason, it is all the more important that we consumers, but video games themselves are protected as a cultural asset by this initiative. I personally have not (yet) been affected by any losses, but considering how large publishers in particular have dealt with their customers so far and plan to deal with them in the foreseeable future, this is only a matter of time. For example, Ubisoft with The Crew, but so EA with its Anthem announced for early 2026, show where the trend is heading; and it is not consumer-friendly. In times where even pure single-player titles are equipped with online compulsion as a feature, so that the control remains available at all times to de facto steal the product back from the buyer without presenting reasons, it is all the more important that initiatives such as the Digital Fairness Act protect the consumer and the work in the long term. IF everything had been presented fairly and Transparently from the outset and a fully-fledged purchase had not been disguised as a license lease in opaque EULAs afterwards, it might have been possible to react earlier. In order to make the purchased software available to consumers even after the publisher’s planned lifetime, a few steps needed to be planned in advance. Technically it is always feasible, but for other reasons its implementation is avoided. The least result should be that publishers clearly communicate whether they are acquiring a product in the sense of a legally valid purchase contract or if it is only a leasing contract. Especially in the case of technical implementations in games, I am aware that the initiative can only have an impact on future events, but it is very important to me that it is at least handled responsibly in the future.
